records.title: CSV → list of dicts
records.description: Read a CSV file into a list of dicts using the header row as keys.
records.code:
import csv
from io import StringIO

def csv_to_dicts(csv_text: str) -> list[dict]:
    reader = csv.DictReader(StringIO(csv_text))
    return list(reader)

sample = """name,age,city
Alice,30,NYC
Bob,25,LA
Carol,35,Chicago"""
print(csv_to_dicts(sample))

records.title: Keyset pagination
records.description: Efficient cursor-based pagination using the last seen ID — avoids OFFSET slowdowns on large tables.
records.code:
-- Forward page: fetch rows after a known cursor
SELECT id, created_at, title
FROM   posts
WHERE  id > :last_seen_id   -- cursor from previous page
ORDER  BY id ASC
LIMIT  :page_size;

-- First page (no cursor)
SELECT id, created_at, title
FROM   posts
ORDER  BY id ASC
LIMIT  :page_size;

records.title: Running total (window)
records.description: Cumulative sum using a window function — no self-join needed.
records.code:
SELECT
    order_date,
    amount,
    SUM(amount) OVER (
        ORDER BY order_date
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
    ) AS running_total
FROM orders
ORDER BY order_date;

records.title: Upsert (INSERT … ON CONFLICT)
records.description: Idempotent insert-or-update for PostgreSQL and SQLite.
records.code:
-- PostgreSQL / SQLite syntax
INSERT INTO user_settings (user_id, theme, locale)
VALUES (:user_id, :theme, :locale)
ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO UPDATE
    SET theme  = EXCLUDED.theme,
        locale = EXCLUDED.locale,
        updated_at = now();

records.title: Find duplicate rows
records.description: Detect rows with duplicate values across one or more columns.
records.code:
-- Find duplicate emails
SELECT email, COUNT(*) AS cnt
FROM   users
GROUP  BY email
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER  BY cnt DESC;

-- Full duplicate rows (all columns identical)
SELECT *, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY col1, col2, col3) AS dup_count
FROM   my_table
QUALIFY dup_count > 1;

records.title: Slugify string
records.description: Convert any string to a URL-safe slug.
records.code:
import re
import unicodedata

def slugify(text: str, separator: str = '-') -> str:
    text = unicodedata.normalize('NFKD', text)
    text = text.encode('ascii', 'ignore').decode('ascii')
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', text).strip().lower()
    return re.sub(r'[-\s]+', separator, text)

print(slugify('Hello, World! This is a Test.'))
print(slugify('Ångström & Co.  2024'))

records.title: Git — prune merged branches
records.description: Delete all local branches that have already been merged into main.
records.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Delete local branches merged into main (safe — skips current & main)
git fetch --prune
git branch --merged main \
  | grep -Ev '^(\*|\s*(main|master|develop)$)' \
  | xargs -r git branch -d
echo 'Done. Remaining branches:'
git branch

records.title: Docker full cleanup
records.description: Remove stopped containers, dangling images, unused volumes and networks.
records.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

echo '=== Stopping all running containers ==='
docker ps -q | xargs -r docker stop

echo '=== Removing containers ==='
docker ps -aq | xargs -r docker rm

echo '=== Removing dangling images ==='
docker images -qf dangling=true | xargs -r docker rmi

echo '=== Pruning volumes + networks ==='
docker volume prune -f
docker network prune -f

echo '=== Done ==='
docker system df

records.title: Token bucket rate limiter
records.description: Thread-safe in-process token bucket — use as Flask/FastAPI middleware or decorator.
records.code:
import threading
import time

class TokenBucket:
    def __init__(self, capacity: int, refill_rate: float):
        """capacity: max tokens; refill_rate: tokens per second."""
        self.capacity = capacity
        self.refill_rate = refill_rate
        self._tokens = float(capacity)
        self._last = time.monotonic()
        self._lock = threading.Lock()

    def consume(self, n: int = 1) -> bool:
        with self._lock:
            now = time.monotonic()
            self._tokens = min(
                self.capacity,
                self._tokens + (now - self._last) * self.refill_rate
            )
            self._last = now
            if self._tokens >= n:
                self._tokens -= n
                return True
            return False

# Example
bucket = TokenBucket(capacity=10, refill_rate=2)
for i in range(15):
    allowed = bucket.consume()
    print(f'Request {i+1:2d}: {"OK" if allowed else "RATE LIMITED"}')
    time.sleep(0.1)
